briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines rather than actual artificial intelligence.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the bankruptcy of fintech middleware company Synapse, highlighting serious risks in banking-as-a-service models.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News* — Fintech innovation is driving increasingly novel and niche credit card products that challenge traditional banking norms.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple has acquired UK-based open banking and credit scoring startup Credit Kudos, signalling the tech giant's deeper ambitions in financial services.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A detailed analysis explores how open banking frameworks are reshaping competition in the payments landscape globally.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[KPMG faces OSC allegations of blown audit in private credit collapse](https://financialpost.com/fp-finance/kpmg-allegations-blown-audit-private-credit-collapse)** — *Financial Post* — The Ontario Securities Commission has alleged that KPMG failed to properly value loans within funds managed by collapsed alternative lender Bridging Finance, raising serious questions about audit oversight in Canada's private credit sector.

- **[NACO wants feds to use $750 million in VC funding to back startups earlier](https://betakit.com/naco-wants-feds-to-use-their-750-million-in-vc-funding-to-back-startups-earlier/)** — *BetaKit* — The National Angel Capital Organization is urging the federal government to deploy its $750 million VC commitment at earlier startup stages to better support Canada's innovation ecosystem.

- **[Canada's economy saw slight growth in January with gains in mining, oil and gas](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/canada-gdp-january-2026-9.7148199?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Statistics Canada reported modest GDP growth in January 2026, driven primarily by the mining, oil, and gas sectors, offering cautious optimism amid ongoing trade uncertainty.

- **[Settlement approved for Canadians affected by past 23andMe data breach](https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/23andme-settlement-class-action-9.7145473?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — A class action settlement has been approved for Canadian victims of the 23andMe data breach, underscoring growing concerns around data privacy and consumer financial protection in Canada.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — *(Included for relevance to Canadian open banking policy context)* Canada signalled its intent to move away from screen scraping toward a formal open banking framework, a regulatory journey that continues to evolve in 2026.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[NACO wants feds to use $750 million in VC funding to back startups earlier](https://betakit.com/naco-wants-feds-to-use-their-750-million-in-vc-funding-to-back-startups-earlier/)** — *BetaKit* — NACO's push for earlier-stage federal VC deployment is particularly relevant to Atlantic Canada, where early-stage fintech and tech startups often struggle to access growth capital compared to larger urban centres.

- **[Fintech dystopia](https://fintechdystopia.com/)** — *Hacker News* — This critical resource cataloguing the darker consequences of unchecked fintech growth serves as a timely reminder for Atlantic Canada's ecosystem builders to prioritize consumer protection and financial inclusion alongside innovation.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — As Canada's open banking framework continues to take shape, this analysis of how open banking drives payments competition is directly relevant to Atlantic Canadian credit unions and fintech startups preparing to participate in a more open financial system.
